Pt orders received for PT eval, chart reviewed, cleared by RN. Pt presents with hypokalemia; see report for details. Pt was
received supine in bed, pleasant and agreeable to PT. Gait belt donned for safety. Pt completed mobility per grid, no LOB,
no fall. Pt Mod I for bed mobility, Mod I STS/transfer and SPV ambulation approx 250ft FWW. Pt declined further mobility.
PT reviewed walker management and upright posture during mobility, VC for attention to walker placement. Pt would
currently benefit from skilled PT during admission to decrease risk for falls and maintain IND. Pt may benefit from skilled
PT post d/c to improve upon functional deficits. Pt reports she has assistance at home and equipment. PT rec pt d/c home
with ongoing skilled therapy, when medically cleared by physician. Pt was left sitting in chair, per pt request, all needs
met, RN updated. Ext time required to complete tasks, rest breaks, slow-steady movements, education. pt declined
toileting and hygiene/ADL at this time. chair alarm on
BARRIERS TO SAFE DC/IMPAIRMENTS AND LIMITATIONS: PMH
THERAPY RECOMMENDATION: PT rec pt d/c home with PT, when medically cleared by physician.

Pt came via walking from pre op c/o low potassium and needing hysterectomy at 0600
this morning. Pt was sent here due to K of 2.4. Pt agitated in triage and only wants food.
Pt has no medical complaints. Pt refused EKG.
Pt AxO4 and ambulatory with personal walker.
Patient is a 81-year-old female with past medical history of abnormal uterine bleeding
and a fib on anticoagulation, was scheduled for hysterectomy was found to have a low
potassium at her PCP prior to scheduled surgery was brought to Valley Hospital
Medical Center on 3/23/2026 to treat hypokalemia. Prior to her surgery scheduled
today was told to follow-up with her PCP. When she followed up with her PCP she was
told she had low potassium and needed to go to the hospital to replete it with IV. The
GynOnc surgeon that was scheduled for the surgery is Dr. Fleury, who originally wanted
to reschedule the surgery for Wednesday, however due to scheduling patient was
told that the surgery needed to be postponed possibly by next week. Patient has been
admitted to Valley Hospital Medical Center to replete potassium. Patient is
asymptomatic denies shortness of breath, chest pain.
Vital signs upon admission were 97.3 °F, 138/63 mmHg, 68 bpm, 100% SpO2 on room
air and 16 breaths/min. Significant labs included blood glucose of 122, potassium of
2.4, creatinine 1.31, TSH of 5.146 UA squamous epithelium of 10, 2+ bacteria, 2+
leukocyte esterase. EKG showed no acute pulmonary process. EKG showed atrial
fibrillation with ST and T wave abnormalities.
PMHx: CHF, Hypertension, Seizures due to prior PEA in 1994
PSHx: Bilateral Lumpectomy for Breast Cancer (Do not place blood pressure cuff on
BACK, BUTTOCK, LEGS AND ARMS BURN

mg daily, ipratropium 21 mcg/inh twice daily, metoprolol tartrate 25 mg twice

Patient is a 81-year-old female with past medical history of abnormal uterine bleeding
and a fib on anticoagulation, was scheduled for hysterectomy was found to have a low
potassium at her PCP prior to scheduled surgery was brought to Valley Hospital
Medical Center on 3/23/2026 to treat hypokalemia. Patient is asymptomatic

Patient is an 81-year-old female with past medical history of paroxysmal A-fib on Plavix,
CHF status post AICD, hypertension, seizure disorder on antiseizure meds who
presented Valley 3/23/2026 for evaluation of hypokalemia. Patient had plan for surgery
today with gyn-onc, plan for hysterectomy due to vaginal bleeding. Patient's potassium
was initially 2.4, most recent 2.8. EKG was completed and did not show flattened T
waves or U waves. It appears to be unchanged from EKG presented by her daughter in
the morning. Patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, dizziness, presyncope.
She states that she has some abdominal/suprapubic cramping due to the vaginal
bleeding. Will replace potassium with IV and oral as tolerated and repeat values. Will
keep patient n.p.o. in case of surgery rescheduled for 3/24/2026, however daughter
mentioned that the surgeon had to reschedule for next Wednesday, next week.
Regarding anticoagulation for paroxysmal A-fib, patient states she was not on an
anticoagulant due to severe GI bleeding in the past. She has been off of Xarelto for a
while and has been taking Plavix since. She denies history of MI or stents.
Will discuss with attending physician, further recommendations to follow.
Attending addendum: I was physically present and involved in the discussion and
management of the patient by the resident, and during critical portions of assessment

General: 81yo Female laying in bed in no acute distress
Mental Status: Alert and oriented to person, place and time. Normal affect.
HEENT: Normocephalic, Atraumatic. PERRL. EOMI. Normal dentition. Oropharynx clear, mucous membranes moist. Ears and nose
without lesions or deformities. Full range of motion. Trachea midline.
Respiratory: CTA bilaterally, no crackles, rales, wheezes, rhonchi.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, rubs or gallops, normal S1, S2, no peripheral edema.
Gastrointestinal: Abdomen soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds normal.
Genitourinary: No CVA tenderness, No suprapubic tenderness.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal neurological deficits.
Skin: No rashes or lesions. No petechiae or purpura. No edema.
Musculoskeletal: No cyanosis or clubbing. No gross deformities. Normal range of motion.
81-year-old female with past medical history of abnormal uterine bleeding and a fib on anticoagulation, was scheduled for hysterectomy
was found to have a low potassium at her PCP prior to scheduled surgery was brought to Valley Hospital Medical Center on 3/23/2026
to treat hypokalemia. Patient surgery was rescheduled for the following week. Upon admission patient hemodynamically stable,
saturating well on room air. Potassium elevated 2.4 EKG showed atrial fibrillation. Patient is not on anticoagulation for her A-fib due to
severe GI bleeding previously. Patient required multiple doses of IV and oral potassium to replete levels reading of 4.0. PT oT evaluated
the patient and determined that patient would benefit from acute rehab following discharge to improve functional
deficits. Recommended the patient follow-up with her PCP regarding hypokalemia.
On discharge, patient has stable vitals, labs within normal limits, and acceptable physical exam. Patient denies any symptoms at this
time. Patient was advised to follow-up with PCP within the next 1-2 weeks. Patient is to return to ED if symptoms worsen or if there are
any questions or concerns. All questions and concerns were answered and addressed at bedside. Patient is currently alert and oriented
to person, place, time, situation. Risk, benefits and alternatives conveyed. Patient has verbalized understanding of diagnoses,
management, treatment, and follow-up instructions. Patient is safe for discharge at this time.
Greater than 30 minutes were spent in the examination, evaluation, counseling, and review of medications and discharge plan for this
This patient will be discussed with the attending physician. Further recommendations to follow.

met, RN updated. Ext time required to complete tasks, rest breaks, slow-steady movements, education. pt declined

General: 81yo Female laying in bed in no acute distress
Mental Status: Alert and oriented to person, place and time. Normal affect.
HEENT: Normocephalic, Atraumatic. PERRL. EOMI. Normal dentition. Oropharynx clear, mucous membranes moist. Ears and nose
without lesions or deformities. Full range of motion. Trachea midline.
Respiratory: CTA bilaterally, no crackles, rales, wheezes, rhonchi.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, rubs or gallops, normal S1, S2, no peripheral edema.
Gastrointestinal: Abdomen soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds normal.
Genitourinary: No CVA tenderness, No suprapubic tenderness.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal neurological deficits.
Skin: No rashes or lesions. No petechiae or purpura. No edema.
Musculoskeletal: No cyanosis or clubbing. No gross deformities. Normal range of motion.
81-year-old female with past medical history of abnormal uterine bleeding and a fib on anticoagulation, was scheduled for hysterectomy
was found to have a low potassium at her PCP prior to scheduled surgery was brought to Valley Hospital Medical Center on 3/23/2026
to treat hypokalemia. Patient surgery was rescheduled for the following week. Upon admission patient hemodynamically stable,
saturating well on room air. Potassium elevated 2.4 EKG showed atrial fibrillation. Patient is not on anticoagulation for her A-fib due to
severe GI bleeding previously. Patient required multiple doses of IV and oral potassium to replete levels reading of 4.0. PT oT evaluated
the patient and determined that patient would benefit from acute rehab following discharge to improve functional
deficits. Recommended the patient follow-up with her PCP regarding hypokalemia.
On discharge, patient has stable vitals, labs within normal limits, and acceptable physical exam. Patient denies any symptoms at this
time. Patient was advised to follow-up with PCP within the next 1-2 weeks. Patient is to return to ED if symptoms worsen or if there are
any questions or concerns. All questions and concerns were answered and addressed at bedside. Patient is currently alert and oriented
to person, place, time, situation. Risk, benefits and alternatives conveyed. Patient has verbalized understanding of diagnoses,
management, treatment, and follow-up instructions. Patient is safe for discharge at this time.
Greater than 30 minutes were spent in the examination, evaluation, counseling, and review of medications and discharge plan for this
This patient will be discussed with the attending physician. Further recommendations to follow.
